text:
# PROCESS — what I did this iteration (explainability; REQUIRED)

## Ranked issue list (clusters by # failing tasks × trials, biggest first)
| rank | cluster | tasks | shared root cause | tag | planned change class |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| 1 | Wrong reservation selected (incomplete lookup) | 1, 2, 8 (flaky 0.90/0.80/0.30) + 39, 42 (always-fail) | Agent checks 1-2 reservations instead of all; picks wrong one by not matching route | BEHAVIORAL | New loop tool get_all_reservation_details + policy |
| 2 | Wrong DOB when replacing passenger | 22 (always-fail) | Agent copies old passenger's DOB onto new person instead of using user's own DOB | KNOWLEDGE | Docstring + policy case distinction |
| 3 | Date-only change substitutes different flight | 10 (flaky 0.10) | Agent searches for new flights when user only asked to move date | KNOWLEDGE | Policy rule + docstring guidance |
| 4 | Communication of refund/charge amounts | 11 (flaky 0.20) + 7 | Agent hand-computes wrong amount or doesn't state it | KNOWLEDGE (output contract) | Policy communication rule |
| 5 | Confirmation stall | 7 (always-fail) | Agent re-asks confirmation when user already gave detailed instructions | BEHAVIORAL + KNOWLEDGE | Policy: treat detailed instructions as confirmation |
| 6 | Payment amount errors in book_reservation | 8 (flaky 0.30) | Agent can't compute exact total; error message unhelpful | KNOWLEDGE | Enriched error message with breakdown |
| 7 | Delay compensation given without change/cancel | 5 (flaky 0.90) | Agent issues certificate for delay without user actually changing/cancelling | KNOWLEDGE | Policy tightening: only after actual change/cancel |
| 8 | Over-cancellation of ineligible reservations | 9 (flaky 0.70) | Agent cancels when no eligibility criterion is met | KNOWLEDGE | Policy reinforcement of "NONE met = refuse" |
| 9 | NYC airport mapping | 42 (always-fail) | Agent can't match "New York" to EWR/JFK/LGA | KNOWLEDGE | Policy additive fact |

## Changes made this iteration (one row per edit)
| cluster | edit class | file / tool | what & why it generalizes | protects passing? |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| 1 (wrong reservation) | compose/add (loop tool) | tools.py / get_all_reservation_details | NEW tool: fetches ALL user reservations in one call. Agent no longer skips reservations. General: any user, any number of reservations. | Yes — additive read-only tool, no existing behavior changed |
| 2 (DOB) | description + policy | tools.py / update_reservation_passengers + policy.md | DOB case distinction: typo→preserve, replace→use new person's DOB. General rule. | Yes — additive knowledge, no tool logic change. Passing tasks that update passengers (none currently) already use correct DOB |
| 3 (date change) | description + policy | tools.py / update_reservation_flights + policy.md | Date-only: keep same flight_number, change only date. Scoped to "user asks to move date" — doesn't affect different-flight searches. | Yes — passing tasks (13, 34) involve different flights/routes, not date-only moves |
| 4 (communication) | policy (output contract) | policy.md | Report ACTUAL charge/refund from tool return value, not hand-calculated. General. | Yes — additive communication rule, no tool behavior change |
| 5 (confirmation) | policy | policy.md | Treat detailed user instructions as confirmation — don't re-ask when user names action+reservation+payment. | Yes — passing tasks already confirm normally; this only loosens the re-ask in cases where user already gave full details |
| 6 (payment errors) | enriched error (code) | tools.py / book_reservation | Payment mismatch error now shows exact breakdown (flights+insurance+baggage=total). Payment_id error shows available methods. | Yes — errors only fire on WRONG amounts/IDs (already-failing); correct payments pass through unchanged |
| 7 (delay compensation) | policy | policy.md | Certificate for delay ONLY after user actually changes/cancels. | Yes — narrowing rule, doesn't affect tasks where compensation is correctly given |
| 8 (over-cancel) | policy | policy.md | Reinforced: do NOT cancel where NONE of 4 criteria met; use get_flight_details to check status. | Yes — additive guidance, passing task 43 already correctly refuses |
| 9 (NYC) | policy knowledge | policy.md | NYC = EWR/JFK/LGA mapping. | Yes — additive factual knowledge |
| 1 (lookup) | policy | policy.md | Updated mandatory steps: use get_all_reservation_details for route-matching; always verify IDs before writes. | Yes — passing tasks already call get_reservation_details |

## Verify-the-fix (one line per change)
- get_all_reservation_details: Task 8 user has 5 reservations, agent only checked 2 in failing trials. New tool returns all 5 in one call → agent can match ORD→PHL. Passing tasks (0,3,4,13,26,28,34,43,46,47) → tool is purely additive read, no write behavior changes.
- DOB case logic: Task 22 gold expects dob='1970-06-06' (Omar's own). New rule: "change to self → use user's DOB from get_user_details." Agent has Omar's DOB (1970-06-06) from get_user_details. Passing tasks that update passengers: none currently.
- Date-only rule: Task 10 gold expects HAT190 on 2024-05-24 (same flight, new date). New rule says keep same flight_number. Passing task 13 involves searching for DIFFERENT flights (nonstop ATL→LAS, not date move) → rule doesn't apply. Task 34 also wants different flight → not affected.
- Communication: Task 11 gold requires stating "5244". New rule: report actual from tool return. Passing tasks already communicate correctly or don't involve price changes requiring communication.
- Confirmation: Task 7 user says "Upgrade XEHM4B to business using card ending 2135, then cancel it. Also cancel 59XX6W." → explicit detailed instruction = confirmation per new rule. Passing tasks: user always gives "yes" in successful trials → no change for them.
- Enriched payment error: Task 8 agent sends wrong amount (500 vs 348). Error now says "Total price is 348 (flights: 258 + insurance: 0 + baggage: 0 + etc), but paid 500. Set payment amounts to sum to exactly 348." Only fires on wrong amounts → no passing task affected (they send correct amounts).
- Delay compensation tightening: Task 5 agent gives certificate without user changing/cancelling. New rule prevents this. Passing tasks with compensation (none in passing set do delay compensation) → unaffected.
- Over-cancel reinforcement: Task 9 agent incorrectly cancels ineligible reservation. Reinforced "NONE met = refuse." Passing task 43 already refuses → behavior preserved.
- NYC mapping: Task 42 agent can't match "New York" → EWR. Knowledge now explicit. Passing tasks not affected.

## Process & features used
- Subagents: 7 parallel diagnostic subagents to analyze different trajectory clusters simultaneously
- Prior iterations read: cand_0001 PROCESS.md + diff.patch, cand_0002 PROCESS.md + diff.patch
- Built on cand_0001: kept ALL its improvements (compute_free_checked_bags, get_flight_details, mandatory first steps, cabin upgrade clarity)
- From cand_0002 (REJECTED): re-introduced DOB case logic, date-only rule, communication rule, NYC mapping, enriched payment error — all edits that did NOT cause the regression. Dropped: cancel_reservation in-body guard (likely caused regression on tasks 13/34/43 by changing the tool's docstring to say "ENFORCES" which made agent try calling it instead of self-refusing)

## Good things to PRESERVE
- get_all_reservation_details loop tool (collapses N calls to 1)
- DOB case-based guidance (self vs typo vs saved passenger)
- Date-only change rule (scoped: only when user asks to move date, not different flight)
- Communication of actual amounts rule
- NYC airport mapping
- All cand_0001 improvements (compute_free_checked_bags, get_flight_details, mandatory first steps, cabin upgrade clarity)
- The cancel_reservation docstring KEEPING "Before calling, verify..." (NOT "This tool ENFORCES") — task 43 passes because the agent self-gates

## Deliberately skipped (cluster + why)
- Task 29 (cancel + rebook under constraints): Complex multi-step task. Without full diagnosis couldn't safely address. NYC mapping may partially help. Will investigate next iteration.
- Task 10 (multi-segment connecting itinerary): Date-only rule helps the simple case, but multi-segment connecting itinerary reconstruction is agent-reasoning-heavy. No safe code fix available without overfitting.
- cancel_reservation in-body guard: Deliberately NOT re-introduced from cand_0002 because it broke tasks 13, 34, 43. The docstring change from "verify before calling" to "ENFORCES" changed agent behavior on task 43 (agent tried calling instead of refusing). Kept current docstring that instructs agent to check BEFORE calling.
